title: Learning Computer Science through real systems
excerpt: How academic fundamentals become more meaningful when they are applied to software, ecommerce, and automation projects.

sections.heading: Fundamentals create better decisions
sections.body: Data structures, algorithms, programming concepts, and system design are not only academic topics. They influence how software is organized, how performance is considered, and how maintainable a solution becomes.

sections.heading: Projects reveal the real constraints
sections.body: Building a working system introduces requirements, edge cases, integration problems, user behavior, and deployment concerns. These constraints make technical learning concrete.

sections.heading: The combination matters
sections.body: My goal is to keep strengthening theory while using it to build practical digital products and workflows that solve understandable problems.
